front: Film: opens with a mortician saying 'I believe in America'; Marlon Brando says 'I'm going to make him an offer he can't refuse'
Film: Michael retrieves a gun from a restaurant bathroom to shoot Officer McCluskey and Sollozzo; climaxes with the baptism intercut with enemy assassinations
Film: ends with a door closing in front of Kay as other men kiss Michael's hand; a studio director wakes up next to a horse's severed head
Film: Vito Corleone puts orange peel in his mouth to chase his grandson in a tomato patch before dying; scored by Nino Rota's Love Theme ('Speak Softly, Now')
Film: helicopters blast Wagner's 'Ride of the Valkyries' during Lieutenant Colonel Kilgore's beach attack; adaptation of Conrad's Heart of Darkness set in Vietnam
Film: when Kurtz first appears, his face is obscured in shadow — only his ear and bald head visible; The Doors' 'The End' plays over the machete killing intercut with a water buffalo slaughter
Film: Walter Murch received the first ever credited Sound Designer credit on this film; its troubled production was documented in Hearts of Darkness

Film: Harry Caul (Gene Hackman) becomes obsessed with the phrase 'he'd kill us if he got the chance'; ends with him playing saxophone in his destroyed apartment after ripping it apart looking for a bug
Film: opens with a descending crane shot of Union Square in San Francisco following a mime; won the Palme d'Or at Cannes 1974; Coppola credited Blow-Up rather than Watergate as its inspiration

Film: a young Vito Corleone is seen as a boy singing to the Statue of Liberty; a character traverses rooftops during a procession of Christ covered in money; Michael recites the Hail Mary while fishing
back: The Godfather Part II (Francis Ford Coppola)

front: Director: won two Palme d'Ors (The Conversation and Apocalypse Now); co-wrote the Oscar-winning screenplay for Patton; lost Best Director to Bob Fosse despite The Godfather winning Best Picture
Director: frequent collaborator with sound designer Walter Murch; co-founded American Zoetrope with George Lucas
